DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT THE MINT B&R, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: (1) GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NUMBERS 8, 9, 11, 13, 15, AND 18. WITH RESPECT TO RESPONSES TO NOS. 9 AND 13, THE COURT NOTES THAT THE RESPONSES WERE VAGUE EITHER AS TO TIME OR AS TO THE PURCHASER. (2) DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NUMBERS 2, 3, 6, 10, 12, 14, AND 16. THE AMENDED RESPONSE CONTAINED SUFFICIENT RESPONSES TO THESE INTERROGATORIES AS PROPOUNDED. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $250. NO SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION EXISTS TO REFUSE TO ANSWER INTERROGATORIES ON THE GROUNDS OF RELEVANCY. AS TO THE BALANCE OF THE GRANTED INTERROGATORIES, SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION EXISTED TO MERIT THE OPPOSITION. COURT WILL PREPARE THE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. ONE AND REQ. FOR SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT THE MINT B&R, INC. DBA THE MINT
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-27-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT THE MINT B&R, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NUMBERS 2.3, 2.5, 6.5, 6.7, AND 8.8. COURT FINDS IN FORM INTERROGATORIES THAT DRIVERS LICENSE INFORMATION MAY LEAD TO SOME RELEVANT EVIDENCE, PARTICULARLY IN VIEW OF THE REQUESTED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 2.5. AS TO NUMBER 2.5, PLAINTIFF SHOULD CLARIFY RESIDENCE ADDRESS DURING THE SUMMER MONTHS OF JUNE AND JULY WHILE PLAINTIFF WAS ON SUMMER VACATION. RESPONSES TO NUMBERS 6.5, 6.7 AND 8.8 ARE ALSO MERITED. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $250. NO SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION EXISTS FOR RELEVANCY OBJECTIONS OR NON-RESPONSES ("DISCOVERY IS ONGOING" IS NOT A SUFFICIENT RESPONSE ON ITS OWN). HOWEVER, MUCH OF THE INFORMATION SOUGHT WAS SUPPLIED IN OTHER RESPONSES, AND FURTHER RESPONSES ARE MORE LIKELY TO DUPLICATE PREVIOUSLY DISCLOSED INFORMATION RATHER THAN PROVIDE SUBSTANTIAL NEW INFORMATION. COURT WILL PREPARE THE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE (TRANSACTION ID # 15027101)

Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Set For Trial On Jan-26-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT THE MINT B&R, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions is Granted in part and denied in part as follows: (1) Granted as to special interrogatory numbers 8, 9, 11, 13, 15, and 18. With respect to responses to nos. 9 and 13, the Court notes that the responses were vague either as to time or as to the purchaser. (2) Denied as to special interrogatory numbers 2, 3, 6, 10, 12, 14, and 16. The amended response contained sufficient responses to these interrogatories as propounded. Request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $250. No substantial justification exists to refuse to answer interrogatories on the grounds of relevancy. As to the balance of the granted interrogatories, substantial justification existed to merit the opposition. Court will prepare the order. Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT THE MINT B&R, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions is Granted as to form interrogatory numbers 2.3, 2.5, 6.5, 6.7, and 8.8. Court finds in form interrogatories that drivers license information may lead to some relevant evidence, particularly in view of the requested responses to form interrogatory no. 2.5. As to number 2.5, plaintiff should clarify residence address during the summer months of June and July while plaintiff was on summer vacation. Responses to numbers 6.5, 6.7 and 8.8 are also merited.
Request for sanctions is Granted in the amount of $250. No substantial justification exists for relevancy objections or non-responses ("Discovery is ongoing" is not a sufficient response on its own). However, much of the information sought was supplied in other responses, and further responses are more likely to duplicate previously disclosed information rather than provide substantial new information.
Court will prepare the order. Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
